families.note: Typed London mulligan, keep, and ordered bottom options are live; sealed benchmark fixtures remain absent.

Every live human and AI priority window uses one shared canonical context with a forced-pass fast path, exact normal-cost instant choices, hierarchical printed-X casting, and typed program-bound non-mana activations. Activation targets, optional answers, announced X, and payments survive the stack; alternate costs, unsupported extra activation costs, and triggered-ability prompts remain open.

Human and AI consume the same canonical land, mana activation, typed program-bound non-mana activation, normal-cost cast, and finish options. Printed X spells defer the numeric announcement and payment into bounded shared subcontexts; descriptors bind exact targets, modes, optional answers, announced X, and every legal payment. Search progressively interleaves typed target families without merging their concrete actions or edge statistics. Additional and alternate costs and spell or trigger resolution-time object choices remain open.

Human and AI consume the same scoped per-attacker contexts, assigning each legal attacker to no attack or any legal live player defender while carrying the typed partial declaration and preserving split multiplayer attacks. Non-player defenders and sealed benchmark fixtures remain open.

Human and AI consume the same scoped per-blocker contexts for every attacked player in deterministic APNAP order; typed partial declarations include fail-closed menace completion and the kernel receives one complete declaration. Non-player defenders and sealed benchmark fixtures remain open.
The live human and AI paths enumerate, validate, dispatch, replay, and record both canonical move-or-leave options; sealed benchmark fixtures remain absent.

Normal spells and typed program-bound activated abilities enumerate exact legal target products and preserve selected targets through stack resolution. Target variants of the same typed spell or ability family share only a progressive-widening key; canonical IDs, legality, transitions, edge statistics, and replay remain concrete. Triggered targets, target distribution, and partial-illegality resolution remain open.
Normal modal spell branches are canonical, visible to humans, available to AI, hashed on the stack, and bound at resolution. Modal activated and triggered abilities remain open.

Printed X spell costs use shared human, heuristic, random, and search contexts with exact replay, typed path identity, hidden-information canaries, and binary narrowing above 64 values. Non-cost numeric choices, variable activated abilities, target distribution, and sealed benchmark labels remain open.

Fixed costs bind explicit mana plans in the root option. Printed X costs use a separate shared Payment context after X is announced, preserve X metadata through cost modification and stack entry, and expose the same final typed CastSpell action to human, heuristic, random, and search consumers. Additional, alternate, and non-mana payment prompts remain open.

Normal spell and typed program-bound activation optional-effect products are canonical, visible to humans, available to AI, hashed on the stack, and bound at resolution. Optional costs and triggered or deferred optional prompts remain open.

Simultaneous same-controller triggers use bounded next-trigger subcontexts inside a kernel-validated APNAP order. Human and AI controllers share membership checks, typed path identity, telemetry, and replay; repeated identical trigger instances are equivalent. Sealed benchmark fixtures and independent acceptable-order labels remain open.

Interpreter object choices now use one scoped canonical context per compiled slot for activated and triggered resolution, retain prior typed selections, and bind actions only after the final slot. Authorized human labels, AI selection, telemetry, and replay share the same path. Non-library breadth, trigger targets, deferred choices, and teacher-corpus fixtures remain incomplete.

Typed program-bound activated and triggered abilities defer object selection until successful resolution and expose one bounded scoped context per search requirement, including fail-to-find and authoritative predicate filtering. Prior selections remain typed and final actions replay canonically without a cross-slot Cartesian product. Spell-resolution, trigger-target, multi-zone, and sealed benchmark coverage remain open.

Live human and AI controllers share bounded next-blocker and sequential amount contexts, including lethal-to-advance, trample, typed path identity, replay telemetry, and logarithmic narrowing for large numeric ranges. The kernel validates the final ordered assignment before mutation; sealed benchmark fixtures and acceptable-action labels remain open.

An explicit human command and AI/benchmark consumers share one hidden-safe singleton Concession context, canonical membership validation, immediate typed kernel action, telemetry descriptor, and exact decision/action replay. Concession is deliberately absent from ordinary random and search action sets; sealed benchmark fixtures remain open.
